tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, with elevated creatinine suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and glucosuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). These findings indicate possible diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology, with evidence of impaired glucose metabolism and possible liver involvement. Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
